title: AI and Machine Learning Jobs
tagline: AI-first and AI-focused roles: ML engineers, applied scientists, LLM engineers, AI product managers. A classifier reads every posting, so roles that merely mention AI stay out.
description: AI and ML jobs updated daily. AI-first and AI-focused roles (ML, LLM, applied science, AI product) classified from 400,000+ crawled listings, with salaries.
keywords: AI-first and AI-focused roles, labeled by a classifier that reads each posting rather than matching keywords
legend: AI focus column: **AI-first** means building AI products or models is the job itself. **AI-focused** means AI/ML is explicitly central to the role.

siblings.label: New Grad Software Engineer Jobs: entry-level roles for graduates
2026–2027 Tech Internships: SWE, data, security, product, and design internships
Remote Tech Jobs: fully remote engineering roles

faq.q: How is this different from other AI job lists?
faq.a: Most lists match the word AI in job titles, which pulls in every role that mentions it. Dreamwork runs a classifier over each of its 400,000+ crawled listings and labels roles AI-first (building AI is the job), AI-focused (AI/ML is explicitly central), or merely AI-enabled. Only the first two appear here.

faq.q: How often is this list updated?
faq.a: Once a day, by a GitHub Action pulling from Dreamwork's public API. Thousands of new AI roles are classified every week; the Dreamwork blog publishes data stories from the same corpus.

faq.q: What kinds of roles show up here?
faq.a: ML engineers, LLM and GenAI engineers, applied and research scientists, AI product managers, AI infrastructure engineers, and AI-native go-to-market roles, at startups and large companies. Use <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>F</kbd> to filter.

faq.q: Can a tool apply to these for me?
faq.a: Yes. Dreamwork matches your resume against every AI listing here, tailors each application, and can submit them for you. Seeing your matches is free.
